chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper describes prototypes implemented on specific versions of memcached and Redis, but it does not provide any working links to public repositories containing the source code or datasets used for evaluation.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The experiments involve implementing modifications (prototypes) onto real-world KVS software (memcached 1.4.39 and Redis 5.0.3) running on a physical server (IBM System x3755 M3). This qualifies as using physical testbeds/real hardware deployment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper has a dedicated section ('C. Fault Model') that clearly defines the scope: targeting memory errors that are detectable but not correctable by existing mechanisms, and explicitly stating what is out of scope (ECC-undetectable errors).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs by measuring Memory Space Overhead (Tables IV & V), Recovery Time (Tables VI & VII), and Runtime Overhead via YCSB benchmark throughput comparisons (Figures 6 & 7).

major_weakness: The paper lacks explicit links to public repositories for the implemented prototypes, hindering full reproducibility.
